Title: Biosynthesis of betalains: yellow and violet plant pigments

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Beta vulgaris gi:34850080; 356968414; 356968424; 356968412; Portulaca grandiflora gi:42558922; Opuntia ficus-indica gi:158714015; 158714117; Mirabilis jalapa gi:209976918; Bougainvillea glabra gi:209976920; Suaeda salsa gi:226510654
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Beta vulgaris; Portulaca grandiflora; Opuntia ficus-indica; Mirabilis jalapa; Bougainvillea glabra; Suaeda salsa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Beta vulgaris; Portulaca grandiflora; Mirabilis jalapa; Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes extradiol ring cleavage of L-DOPA to 4,5-seco-DOPA, which spontaneously condenses to betalamic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the immediate precursor of betalamic acid, the structural chromophoric unit of betalains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides yellow and violet coloration; antioxidant activity; chemopreventive/health-promoting potential
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers; fruits; roots; leaves; colored tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected mainly in developing colored flowers of Mirabilis jalapa, particularly at final stages; in betalain-forming plants grouped apart phylogenetically
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: White petal cells of Portulaca grandiflora gained pigmentation after complementation; constitutive expression conferred color in Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes betalamic acid production and enables betalain pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biolistic genetic complementation in Portulaca grandiflora; recombinant protein expression and in vitro enzyme assay in Mirabilis jalapa; enzyme purification and kinetic characterization in Beta vulgaris; transient/constitutive heterologous expression
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Considered the characteristic/pivotal enzyme of betalain biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DODA converts L-DOPA into 4,5-seco-DOPA, leading to betalamic acid formation. 2. This enzyme is presented as the characteristic and pivotal step in betalain-producing Caryophyllales. 3. Functional complementation, recombinant assays, and purified enzyme studies support its causal role in betalain pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review article. Plant DODAs involved in betalain formation show only 4,5-extradiol activity, unlike Amanita muscaria enzyme that also has 2,3-dioxygenase activity producing muscaflavin-related products. Conserved residues near the active site may favor spontaneous condensation to betalamic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering betalain production in heterologous species because it provides the core chromophoric unit and was sufficient to induce pigmentation in complementation/heterologous expression contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Sequence/phylogenetic analysis from GenBank accessions listed in review
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive promoter in Arabidopsis thaliana; transient/biolistic expression systems in Portulaca grandiflora

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Portulaca grandiflora; Beta vulgaris; Suaeda salsa; Phytolacca americana; Spinacia oleracea
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Portulaca grandiflora; Beta vulgaris; Suaeda salsa; Phytolacca americana; Spinacia oleracea
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Portulaca grandiflora; Beta vulgaris; Suaeda salsa; Phytolacca americana
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type-3 copper polyphenol oxidase / tyrosinase family
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: L-DOPA formation and oxidative branch toward cyclo-DOPA/betanidin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates L-tyrosine to L-DOPA and oxidizes o-diphenols such as L-DOPA, dopaxanthin, and betanidin to the corresponding o-quinones
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and drives oxidative conversion toward betacyanin intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Betalains (especially betacyanins and some betaxanthin intermediates)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides yellow/violet coloration; antioxidant and health-promoting activities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcripts detected in fruits of Phytolacca americana correlating with betacyanin accumulation; activity related to pigment accumulation in Beta vulgaris and Suaeda salsa
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Correlates with colored tissues and pigment accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes precursor formation and oxidative reactions needed for betalain biosynthesis, especially betacyanin-related branch chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme characterization from betalain-forming plants; transcript correlation with pigment accumulation; activity assays; biochemical substrate conversion studies
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important control point but not established as sole key bottleneck
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tyrosinase catalyzes multiple proposed steps in betalain biosynthesis, including L-tyrosine hydroxylation to L-DOPA. 2. It can also oxidize L-DOPA and betalain intermediates such as dopaxanthin and betanidin. 3. Transcript/activity correlations suggest regulated contribution to pigment formation in precise tissues and stages.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its exact biosynthetic contribution remains partly unresolved because several proposed reactions may alternatively involve other enzymes; latent enzyme activation by proteases/activators reported in Beta vulgaris. No sequence signature specific for betalain-producing plants identified.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for modulating precursor supply and betacyanin branch flux, but deployment requires caution because oxidation chemistry can also consume intermediates and promote browning/polymer formation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Sequence information for plant tyrosinases limited; transcript analyses reported
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Presence or absence of reducing agents such as ascorbic acid strongly affects pathway outcome
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can consume L-DOPA and promote enzymatic browning/polymer formation if reducing environment is not maintained

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Dorotheanthus bellidiformis gi:5918023; 18033791; Beta vulgaris gi:29692096; 46430997; 46430995
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Dorotheanthus bellidiformis; Beta vulgaris; Mirabilis jalapa
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Dorotheanthus bellidiformis; Beta vulgaris; Mirabilis jalapa
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the 5-hydroxyl group of betanidin to form betanin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms a major stable betacyanin end product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to violet coloration and pigment stabilization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript accumulation correlates with pigment formation in Beta vulgaris; glucosyltransferase activity/transcripts correlate positively with betanin accumulation in developing flowers of Mirabilis jalapa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation and stabilization of betanin from betanidin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purification and characterization; homology modeling and site-directed mutagenesis; antisense constructs in Beta vulgaris; activity assays correlated with pigment accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream decorating step; transcriptionally regulated
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Betanidin glucosyltransferase converts betanidin into betanin by glucosylation. 2. This modification stabilizes betacyanin pigments. 3. Transcript accumulation and antisense evidence in Beta vulgaris support a causal role in betanin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Other related glucosyltransferases from Beta vulgaris glucosylate betanidin less efficiently than other substrates. A 6-O-glucosyltransferase from Dorotheanthus bellidiformis forms gomphrenin I instead.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering stable betacyanin accumulation and tuning pigment composition after introducing upstream betalain biosynthetic capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Database deposition of multiple glucosyltransferase sequences; transcript studies
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Antisense constructs used in Beta vulgaris

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Beta vulgaris gi:356968415; Amaranthus cruentus gi:356968419; Mirabilis jalapa gi:356968421
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Beta vulgaris; Amaranthus cruentus; Mirabilis jalapa
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Beta vulgaris; Amaranthus cruentus; Mirabilis jalapa
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase involved in cyclo-DOPA formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-DOPA into cyclo-DOPA by an oxidative mechanism not fully resolved
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for betanidin/betacyanin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cyclo-DOPA production and supports betacyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic complementation and gene silencing in Beta vulgaris
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key alternative oxidative step for cyclo-DOPA formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A cytochrome P450 from Beta vulgaris converts L-DOPA to cyclo-DOPA. 2. Genetic complementation and silencing support its role in betacyanin biosynthesis. 3. Homologs occur in other betalain-forming plants, suggesting a conserved specialized branch enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as the first member of a new P450 subfamily; reaction mechanism remains unresolved and is shown separately from tyrosinase-mediated oxidation in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important candidate for engineering betacyanin branch flux together with DODA and glucosyltransferase genes.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Sequence homologs reported with GenBank accessions

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the 6-hydroxyl group of betanidin to form gomphrenin I
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms an alternative betacyanin product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A 6-O-glucosyltransferase from Dorotheanthus bellidiformis forms gomphrenin I from betanidin. 2. It demonstrates alternative glucosylation positions diversify betacyanin structures.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in review as a core summarized enzyme for betalain diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to diversify betacyanin profiles in metabolic engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Mirabilis jalapa gi:62086401; Celosia cristata gi:62086403
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Alternative betacyanin biosynthetic branch via cyclo-DOPA glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to cyclo-DOPA, producing cyclo-DOPA-glucoside that can subsequently condense with betalamic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an alternative immediate precursor to betanin-like betacyanins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activity and transcript changes correlate with betanin accumulation in developing flowers of Mirabilis jalapa
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports an alternative route to betacyanin formation through cyclo-DOPA glucosylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme characterization and activity assays in Mirabilis jalapa and Celosia cristata
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Alternative pathway step; not universally established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glucosyltransferase activity on cyclo-DOPA rather than betanidin was characterized in Mirabilis jalapa. 2. Similar activity was reported in Celosia cristata. 3. These findings support an alternative route to betacyanin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes this route contrasts with the generally accepted direct glucosylation of betanidin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May enable alternative pathway design for specific betacyanin derivatives.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes betanin back to betanidin by removing glucose
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for turnover/catabolism of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potentially reduces stable betanin and may contribute to pigment catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical activity report cited in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. β-glucosidase can convert betanin back to betanidin. 2. The physiological relevance is unclear, but a role in pigment catabolism is proposed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because review explicitly summarizes its metabolic role in betalain turnover.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target to suppress for stabilizing betanin accumulation, though physiological role remains uncertain.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unknown due to unclear physiological role

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Betacyanin oxidation and decarboxylated derivative formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Betanin phenoxy radical; betanidin-quinone and dehydrogenated/decarboxylated derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes betanin and betanidin through radical mechanisms, leading to oxidized, dehydrogenated, decarboxylated, and hydrolyzed derivatives
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with/stimulates degradation or remodeling of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes oxidation and diversification/degradation of betacyanins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical oxidation studies summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Peroxidase oxidizes betanin and betanidin. 2. The resulting radicals/quinones can produce dehydrogenated and decarboxylated betalain derivatives. 3. These reactions may contribute both in planta and as extraction artifacts.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Relevant particularly to interpretation of decarboxylated betalain formation and pigment stability.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential suppression target to improve pigment stability, but physiological roles beyond betalains are likely broad.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic because peroxidases have broad physiological functions
